title: I’m From a Country FIFA Banned — I Watched the Final From a $120K VIP Suite
excerpt: My passport is on FIFA’s restricted list. I couldn’t buy a $50 ticket. But I sat in a Chairman’s Suite at the Final. Here’s the loophole nobody talks about.
content:
My passport says I shouldn’t be here.

I’m from one of the 7 countries on FIFA’s restricted list for 2026. Officially, I can’t buy a single match ticket. Not a $50 group stage seat. Not a $200 quarter-final.

Nothing.

So how did I end up in a Chairman’s Suite at the Final?

## The Loophole

FIFA’s restriction applies to tickets. It does NOT apply to hospitality packages.

Match tickets are sold through FIFA’s portal, which blocks certain passport numbers. Hospitality packages are sold through independent vendors who handle their own verification.

Private concierge services don’t check your passport against FIFA’s list. They check your wallet.

That’s the loophole.

## How It Actually Worked

I found Vantage 26 through a recommendation. Sent them my passport scan (yes, from the banned country). They replied in 4 hours:

“Not a problem. Which match?”

I picked the Final. They sent me a crypto payment address. I transferred the amount. 12 minutes later, I had a digital confirmation.

No embassy. No appeal. No legal fees.

## At the Stadium

The morning of the Final, I kept waiting for someone to stop me.

At the private entrance: “Good evening, sir. Right this way.”

At the suite door: “Welcome. Champagne?”

Nobody checked my passport. Nobody asked where I was from.

I was just another guest in a suite.

## The Real Story

The irony isn’t lost on me. FIFA doesn’t want my money for a $50 ticket. But they’re happy to let me spend $120,000 on a suite.

And the VIP experience is better anyway.
